metadata.title: Human vs AI Trivia Challenge - Question Bank
metadata.author: dc_dalin

questions.question: What is the capital of France?
questions.explanation: Paris has been the capital of France since 987 AD.

questions.question: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers about 46% of Earth's water surface.

questions.question: Who wrote Romeo and Juliet?
questions.explanation: William Shakespeare wrote this tragic play around 1594-1596.

questions.question: What year did World War II end?
questions.explanation: WWII ended in 1945 with Germany's surrender in May and Japan's in September.

questions.question: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa between 1503-1519.

questions.question: In which year did the Titanic sink?
questions.explanation: The RMS Titanic sank on April 15, 1912 after hitting an iceberg.

questions.question: How many sides does a hexagon have?
questions.explanation: A hexagon is a polygon with six sides and six angles.

questions.question: What is 15 × 8?
questions.explanation: 15 multiplied by 8 equals 120.

questions.question: What is the square root of 144?
What is the smallest prime number?
questions.explanation: 2 is the smallest and only even prime number.

questions.question: How many minutes are in a day?
questions.explanation: 24 hours × 60 minutes = 1440 minutes.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word 'aurum' meaning gold.

questions.question: How many planets are in our solar system?
questions.explanation: There are 8 planets since Pluto was reclassified in 2006.

questions.question: What is the speed of light in vacuum (in million m/s)?
questions.explanation: Light travels at approximately 299,792,458 m/s or ~300 million m/s.

questions.question: What does HTTP stand for?
questions.explanation: HTTP is the foundation of data communication on the World Wide Web.

questions.question: What does RMSLE stand for in machine learning evaluation?
questions.explanation: RMSLE is used to evaluate predictions where underestimates and overestimates matter differently.

questions.question: What technique adds trainable low-rank matrices to frozen LLM layers?
questions.explanation: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique.

questions.question: What is the minimum number of training examples for OpenAI fine-tuning?
questions.explanation: OpenAI requires at least 10 examples, though 50-100 is recommended for best results.

questions.question: What does RAG stand for in LLM applications?
questions.explanation: RAG combines retrieval systems with generation models to provide context-aware responses.

questions.question: Which Meta model is commonly used for fine-tuning?
questions.explanation: Llama 3.1 is Meta's latest open-source model family popular for fine-tuning.

questions.question: What file format is used for OpenAI fine-tuning data?
questions.explanation: JSONL (JSON Lines) has one JSON object per line, making it easy to stream large datasets.

questions.question: What tokenizer type do most modern LLMs use?
questions.explanation: BPE (Byte Pair Encoding) balances vocabulary size and token granularity.

questions.question: What does QLoRA's Q stand for?
questions.explanation: QLoRA combines quantization with LoRA to reduce memory usage during fine-tuning.

questions.question: What embedding model family is known for strong retrieval performance?
questions.explanation: E5 embeddings from Microsoft are optimized for text retrieval tasks.

questions.question: Which library is commonly used for vector databases in Python?
questions.explanation: ChromaDB is a popular open-source embedding database for AI applications.

questions.question: What is the boiling point of water in Celsius?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100°C at standard atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: Who developed the theory of relativity?
questions.explanation: Einstein published the theory of special relativity in 1905 and general relativity in 1915.

questions.question: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet with a mass greater than all other planets combined.

questions.question: What is the most spoken language in the world?
questions.explanation: English is the most widely spoken language globally when including non-native speakers.

questions.question: How many continents are there?
questions.explanation: The seven continents are Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Antarctica, Europe, and Australia.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world?
questions.explanation: Vatican City has an area of only 0.44 km².

questions.question: Who invented the telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell patented the telephone in 1876.

questions.question: What is the currency of Japan?
questions.explanation: The Japanese yen (¥) has been Japan's currency since 1871.

questions.question: How many keys does a standard piano have?
questions.explanation: A standard piano has 88 keys (52 white and 36 black).

questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.explanation: Diamond ranks 10 on the Mohs hardness scale.

questions.question: Which famous painting features a woman with a mysterious smile?
questions.explanation: The Mona Lisa's enigmatic smile has fascinated viewers for centuries.

questions.question: What is 2 to the power of 10?
questions.explanation: 2^10 = 1024, which is also 1 kilobyte in binary.

questions.question: What does CPU stand for?
questions.explanation: The CPU is the brain of a computer that executes instructions.

questions.question: In what year was the first iPhone released?
questions.explanation: Steve Jobs unveiled the first iPhone on January 9, 2007.

questions.question: What programming language is known for its use in data science?
questions.explanation: Python's simplicity and rich ecosystem make it ideal for data science.

questions.question: What does PEFT stand for in machine learning?
questions.explanation: PEFT methods like LoRA reduce the number of trainable parameters during fine-tuning.

questions.question: Which metric penalizes underestimation more than overestimation?
questions.explanation: RMSLE (Root Mean Squared Logarithmic Error) is more forgiving of overestimates.

questions.question: What library is used for efficient transformer model training?
questions.explanation: HuggingFace Transformers provides pre-trained models and training utilities.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using embeddings in RAG?
questions.explanation: Embeddings enable semantic similarity search beyond keyword matching.

questions.question: What does the rank r represent in LoRA?
questions.explanation: The rank r determines the dimensionality of the decomposed weight matrices in LoRA.

questions.question: Which ancient wonder is the only one still standing?
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the oldest and only surviving ancient wonder.

questions.question: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River is approximately 6,650 km long.

questions.question: Who wrote '1984'?
questions.explanation: George Orwell published '1984' in 1949 as a dystopian novel.

questions.question: What is the value of pi to two decimal places?
questions.explanation: Pi (π) is approximately 3.14159, rounded to 3.14.

questions.question: What does API stand for?
questions.explanation: An API allows different software applications to communicate with each other.
